data.narrative:
HyImpulse, a Series B space and aerospace company founded by Mario Kobald, has raised €74M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 3 same-stage private peers in Space & Aerospace (Aalyria, Hubble Network, and Sift), supplemented by reference to 5 public sector comps (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

The peer-median method provides the primary triangulation anchor. The three Series B comparables span a wide range of $274M to $1.3B in valuation, with Aalyria at $1.3B, Hubble Network at $400M, and Sift at $274M. The median across this set is $400M. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (sector median of 59.8x) cannot be applied here, as HyImpulse's revenue is not disclosed—a common constraint in private company valuation.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $340M to $460M, centered on the peer median of $400M. The wide dispersion among private comparables ($274M–$1.3B) reflects material differences in technology maturity, customer traction, or market positioning within the sector. Key uncertainties include HyImpulse's revenue run-rate, customer concentration, and technological differentiation relative to the three peers. Any Series B valuation should be stress-tested against both the lower tail (Sift) and upper tail (Aalyria) to account for potential positioning variance.
